question_text: Let A be a $3 \times 3$ real matrix such that $ A \left[ 1 \right] = \left[ { \begin{array} { c c c c c c c } { 1 } \\ { 0 } \\ { 4 } \\ { 0 } \end{array} } \right] , \quad A \left[ 1 \right] = \left[ { \begin{array} { c c c c c c c } { 0 } \\ { 0 } \\ { 1 } \\ { 0 } \end{array} } \right] , \quad A \left[ 0 \right] = \left[ { \begin{array} { c c c c c c c } { 1 } \\ { 0 } \\ { 0 } \\ { 1 } \end{array} } \right] . $ Then which of the following statements is/are true?
solution:
The given problem provides the results of multiplying matrix A with specific vectors. Based on this information, we can deduce the matrix A. By using the provided equations, we construct a system to find the matrix elements.

 $ A = { \left[ \begin{array} { l l l } { 2 } & { 2 } & { - 2 } \\ { 2 } & { - 2 } & { 2 } \\ { 8 } & { 4 } & { 0 } \end{array} \right] } $ 

Now, checking the options:

(A) We check if multiplying A with $\left[ 1 \right] \atop { \mathrm { g i v e s } } \left[ \begin{array} { l } { 2 } \\ { 2 } \\ { 2 } \\ { - 2 } \end{array} \right]$ , which it does.   
Thus, the correct answer is (A).
Quick Tip

\- To solve matrix-related problems, first find the matrix elements using the given vectors and their corresponding outcomes.

\- Verify each option by performing the matrix multiplication and comparing the results.
